hero.headline: NMIMS Online MBA in Marketing Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved specialisation. 14 spec subjects across Sem 3-4. NIRF #52, Mumbai heritage brand.

sections.tldr: NMIMS Online MBA in Marketing Management covers brand management, consumer behaviour, integrated marketing communications, sales management, international marketing, services marketing, and digital marketing across 14 Sem 3-4 subjects. NIRF #52 at Rs 1,96,000. Best for marketing professionals targeting brand manager, marketing head, or growth leadership roles.

about.body:
NMIMS (Narsee Monjee Institute of Management Studies) has a strong marketing heritage built through its Mumbai campus, which has placed brand managers and marketing directors at FMCG companies, consumer tech firms, and media agencies. The NMIMS Online MBA in Marketing Management brings that brand and curriculum to working professionals.

The Marketing Management specialisation covers the full marketing management scope: brand equity building, consumer behaviour analysis, integrated communications, field sales management, international marketing strategy, services marketing, and digital marketing. The combination of classical brand management with international marketing and digital channels makes this a well-rounded marketing credential.

This specialisation suits marketing executives and brand managers with 2-5 years of experience who want to formalise their expertise, move from execution roles to marketing leadership, and carry a brand name that FMCG and consumer company hiring managers recognise. Full programme overview at /universities/nmims-online/mba.

sem3Subjects.name: Corporate Finance
sem3Subjects.description: Build financial literacy for marketers: P&L management, marketing ROI measurement, brand investment evaluation, and budget justification skills.

sem3Subjects.name: Research Methodology
sem3Subjects.description: Apply market research design, survey instruments, consumer panel data, and hypothesis testing to generate insights for marketing decisions.

sem3Subjects.name: Project Pre-work
sem3Subjects.description: Define dissertation scope, conduct literature review, and establish research questions for the Sem 4 marketing management capstone project.

sem3Subjects.name: Brand Management
sem3Subjects.description: Build brand equity through positioning strategy, brand architecture decisions, brand extension analysis, and brand health tracking systems.

sem3Subjects.name: Consumer Behaviour
sem3Subjects.description: Analyse psychological drivers of purchase decisions, social influence mechanisms, buyer journey mapping, and segmentation strategy inputs.

sem3Subjects.name: Integrated Marketing Communications
sem3Subjects.description: Design consistent multi-channel campaigns combining ATL, BTL, digital, PR, and direct marketing with unified messaging and measurement.

sem3Subjects.name: Sales Management
sem3Subjects.description: Build and manage field sales teams: territory design, target-setting systems, sales force training, incentive structures, and distributor management.

sem4Subjects.name: Indian Ethos and Ethics
sem4Subjects.description: Apply Indian philosophical and ethical frameworks to marketing practice including advertising ethics, fair pricing, and responsible brand communication.

sem4Subjects.name: Corporate Sustainability
sem4Subjects.description: Integrate ESG principles into brand strategy, develop purpose-led marketing campaigns, and communicate sustainability credentials to consumers.

sem4Subjects.name: International Business
sem4Subjects.description: Analyse global market entry strategies, cross-border brand management, and adapting marketing mix elements across diverse international markets.

sem4Subjects.name: Project
sem4Subjects.description: Complete a dissertation-grade research or strategy project applying Marketing Management specialisation knowledge to a real brand or market challenge.

sem4Subjects.name: International Marketing
sem4Subjects.description: Design marketing strategies for international expansion: standardisation vs adaptation decisions, global consumer segmentation, and export channel management.

sem4Subjects.name: Services Marketing
sem4Subjects.description: Apply the 7Ps of services marketing, manage the GAP model for service quality, and design customer experience frameworks for service organisations.

sem4Subjects.name: Digital Marketing
sem4Subjects.description: Integrate SEO, paid media, social media strategy, content marketing, and marketing automation into a full-funnel digital marketing programme.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1 and 2 cover the shared management core including Marketing Management (Sem 1), Business Analytics, Strategic Management, and HR Management. See the main NMIMS MBA page for Sem 1-2 subject details.

whoHires.body:
Marketing Management MBA graduates from NMIMS Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** FMCG, Consumer technology, Digital marketing agencies, Media companies, D2C brands

**Entry-level roles:** Brand Executive (management track), Marketing Manager, Digital Marketing Manager, Category Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Brand Manager, Senior Brand Manager, Head of Digital Marketing, Trade Marketing Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** Marketing Director, VP Marketing, CMO, Group Brand Manager

**Top hiring organisations:** HUL, P&G India, Nestle India, Mondelez, Reckitt, Dabur, Marico, Godrej Consumer Products, ITC FMCG, GroupM, Publicis India, Swiggy, Zomato

skills.body:
The NMIMS Marketing Management specialisation builds skills that FMCG and consumer brand employers test at brand manager and marketing head levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Brand Management: brand equity measurement using brand funnel and NPS, positioning statement development, brand architecture decisions, and brand extension risk assessment for FMCG interviews
- Consumer Behaviour: buyer persona development, purchase decision tree analysis, category driver identification, and consumer insight translation into creative brief
- Integrated Marketing Communications: ATL and BTL channel planning, media mix optimisation, campaign brief writing, and unified communication measurement for brand campaigns
- Sales Management: field sales team structure, distributor management, secondary sales tracking, and sales incentive design for FMCG national sales roles

**Applied skills:**
- International Marketing: global brand adaptation strategy, distribution in export markets, and cultural insight for cross-border FMCG and consumer campaigns
- Digital Marketing: digital channel integration, SEO fundamentals, social media strategy, and performance marketing basics for brand campaign management

**Soft skills:** Writing creative briefs for external agencies, presenting brand health data to senior management, and defending marketing investment against finance teams in P&L reviews.

comparisons.body:
Comparing NMIMS Online MBA Marketing Management against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA Marketing and Sales Management:** Amity is NAAC A+ and NIRF #22 at Rs 2,25,000. Amity Marketing and Sales covers sales management and communications broadly. NMIMS Marketing Management adds dedicated International Marketing, Services Marketing, and Brand Management depth. For FMCG brand management careers in Mumbai, NMIMS brand recognition is stronger. For pan-India marketing and sales management roles at mid-size companies, Amity NIRF #22 is competitive.

**vs Symbiosis Online MBA Marketing:** Symbiosis is NAAC A+ at competitive fees with strong recognition in the Pune-Maharashtra corporate ecosystem, particularly for Bajaj, Kirloskar, and Pune-based FMCG companies. NMIMS carries stronger recognition at Mumbai-headquartered FMCG and consumer technology employers. Choose based on target employer geography and sector.

**vs Chandigarh University Online MBA Marketing Management:** Chandigarh University is NAAC A++ with active placement infrastructure in north India. For marketing management roles at north India-based consumer companies and retail, Chandigarh University has a strong campus placement network. NMIMS carries significantly stronger brand recognition at pan-India FMCG companies and the Mumbai marketing ecosystem.

fees.body:
The NMIMS Online MBA in Marketing Management offers 3 payment options:

**Option 1 — Annual payment**: Rs 1,05,000 per year, Rs 2,10,000 total for 2 years
**Option 2 — Semester-wise**: Rs 55,000 per semester, Rs 2,20,000 total for 4 semesters
**Option 3 — Full upfront payment (discounted)**: Rs 1,96,000 one-time

An Admission Processing Fee of Rs 1,200 applies to all admissions. Rs 10,000 is collected at registration and counts toward the program fee. Examination fee is Rs 800 per subject per attempt, and project evaluation fee is Rs 800 per attempt.

For working professionals, NMIMS offers no-cost EMI plans through NBFC lending partners. Contact our counsellor for current EMI options and approval terms.

**Scholarship**: Armed Forces, Paramilitary personnel, and their dependents receive a 20 percent discount on the program fee. Submit service verification documents during admission to claim this.

Demand Draft payments should be made in favour of 'SVKM's NMIMS' payable at Mumbai.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with NMIMS each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The NMIMS (Narsee Monjee Institute of Management Studies) degree certificate carries the NMIMS name, the MBA designation, and identifies the online learning mode per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The Marketing Management specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode identification on the certificate is standard for UGC-DEB approved programmes and equivalent in recognition to regular MBA degrees for employment, promotions, and further studies.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from NMIMS Online MBA Marketing Management alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Sneha Patel
items.liked: Brand management module was the most thorough brand equity framework I had encountered — directly applicable to my FMCG brand work
items.disliked: Services marketing module could benefit from more India-specific healthcare and edtech service marketing examples
items.body: I was an assistant brand manager at an FMCG company targeting a brand manager promotion that required a management degree. The NMIMS Marketing Management spec was the right credential — my interviewer at the new company specifically asked about it and was familiar with NMIMS curriculum quality. The brand equity and consumer behaviour content gave me frameworks I now use weekly. Got the Brand Manager role 4 months after completing the MBA.

items.name: Ankit Sharma
items.liked: Sales management module covered distributor management and secondary sales systems that most marketing MBAs ignore
items.disliked: Digital marketing module was introductory — professionals already doing performance marketing needed more advanced content
items.body: I manage a regional sales team at an FMCG company. The sales management and trade marketing content was substantive — the distributor management and territory design frameworks were directly applicable. The NMIMS degree helped me qualify for the National Sales Manager role that my company lists as requiring an MBA from a recognised institution. NMIMS was specifically verified by our Group HR.

items.name: Pooja Menon
items.liked: Integrated marketing communications module unified my understanding of ATL, BTL, and digital channel planning
items.disliked: International marketing content was weighted toward export management — more global brand management strategy content would be valuable
items.body: I work in marketing at a consumer technology company. The IMC and consumer behaviour content were the strongest sections. The brand equity measurement frameworks I learned during the MBA changed how I report brand health to leadership. The NMIMS Marketing brand was recognised by the CMO during my promotion review — she is an NMIMS campus alumna and validated the programme's rigour.

faqs.question: Is this MBA better for FMCG brand management or digital marketing careers?
faqs.answer: NMIMS Marketing Management is stronger for FMCG brand management, where the NMIMS brand name is well-recognised by hiring managers who are often NMIMS campus alumni. For pure digital marketing careers, supplement the MBA with Google and Meta certifications. The IMC, brand management, and consumer behaviour subjects are more valuable for FMCG brand roles. The digital marketing Sem 4 subject gives digital channel context but is not a digital marketing specialisation by itself.

faqs.question: Does this MBA cover FMCG trade marketing and modern retail management?
faqs.answer: Sales Management in Sem 3 covers distributor management, field sales, and trade channel management which is directly relevant to FMCG trade marketing. Consumer Behaviour includes retail shopper decision-making context. For deep modern retail trade marketing, also look at the Integrated Marketing Communications content. The curriculum is substantive for FMCG professionals but does not have a dedicated trade marketing module.

faqs.question: How much does the NMIMS campus brand carry over to the online MBA degree?
faqs.answer: The online MBA carries the NMIMS (Narsee Monjee Institute of Management Studies) name on the degree — the same name as the campus degree. In practice, FMCG and consumer brand HR managers in Mumbai recognise the NMIMS name from their own campus recruitment history. The brand is strongest in the BFSI and FMCG sectors in Mumbai, and resonates with hiring managers who are NMIMS alumni. Outside Mumbai, NMIMS brand recognition is good but not as differentiated.

faqs.question: What salary can NMIMS Marketing MBA graduates expect?
faqs.answer: Brand managers at FMCG companies with 3-5 years of experience and this MBA typically earn Rs 10-20 lakh. Marketing managers at consumer tech companies earn Rs 12-22 lakh. Senior brand managers and marketing directors at large FMCG organisations see Rs 18-40 lakh. The degree most significantly impacts career advancement when it qualifies a specialist for a management role that formally requires an MBA.

faqs.question: Can I switch from a non-marketing background to marketing using this MBA?
faqs.answer: Yes, with caveats. The Sem 1-2 marketing core builds the foundation. For career switchers, the NMIMS brand is a strong entry point but FMCG brand management roles are competitive and typically prefer candidates with 1-2 years of marketing exposure. Consider starting in a marketing analytics, digital marketing, or trade marketing role after completing Sem 1-2 while finishing the degree — practical experience alongside the credential is more effective than waiting for degree completion.

chooseIf: You are a marketing professional targeting FMCG brand management, marketing leadership, or IMC strategy roles and want the NMIMS name specifically recognised in Mumbai's consumer brand ecosystem.
You want comprehensive marketing coverage from brand management and consumer behaviour through sales, international marketing, and digital channels in one specialisation.
You need a NIRF-ranked management degree with a marketing specialisation that carries brand weight at FMCG companies and consumer tech firms.
consider: You are primarily targeting digital marketing, performance marketing, or growth marketing roles — Amity Online's Digital Marketing Management spec has more digital channel depth.
You want a broader cross-functional MBA credential beyond marketing — General Management or NMIMS Business Management gives more management breadth.
You are targeting premium marketing strategy roles at MBB consulting firms — an IIM or ISB programme is the relevant benchmark for those specific hiring pipelines.
